items.title: Class interface and invariants: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Encapsulation means adding getters and setters for every private field, regardless of invariants.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Expose domain operations, not automatic field access; each public operation should preserve the class invariant and hide replaceable representation.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Expose domain operations, not automatic field access; each public operation should preserve the class invariant and hide replaceable representation.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Expose domain operations, not automatic field access; each public operation should preserve the class invariant and hide replaceable representation.
Encapsulation means adding getters and setters for every private field, regardless of invariants.

answer.explanation: Expose domain operations, not automatic field access; each public operation should preserve the class invariant and hide replaceable representation.

items.title: Class interface and invariants: complete the language rule

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'class invariant'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Expose domain operations, not automatic field access; each public operation should preserve the class invariant and hide replaceable representation.

items.title: Class interface and invariants: executable application
items.prompt: Implement Account with a private nonnegative balance, deposit and withdraw operations that reject negative amounts and overdrafts, then print the resulting balance or rejected.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: private invariant protected by public operations

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises private invariant protected by public operations under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
